reachability_artifacts: stderr demonstration: implement attempt N timed out after <d> — escalating
open_deferrals: default http.Client.Timeout on internal/model/oai.go — deferred; per-attempt ctx deadline already bounds the HTTP call, and oai.go is a future S39/T5 touchpoint (Rule 2, Coach ack 2026-06-21)
killing agent-spawned OS subprocesses — deferred; supervisor stale-PID reaping covers cross-session orphans (Rule 2, Coach ack 2026-06-21)

design_decisions.choice: Named constant DefaultImplementTimeout = 15 * time.Minute in internal/run/slice.go — single tuning point per spec's Risks section
options: Magic number 15m at each call site
design_decisions.rationale: A single named constant makes tuning trivial and prevents scattered magic numbers. spec.md Risks instructs 'make it a single named constant for easy tuning.'

design_decisions.choice: context.DeadlineExceeded detection via errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) on implement.Run return — differentiates timeout from other implementer errors for stderr message, but escalation path is identical.
options: Differentiate DeadlineExceeded for distinct stderr message
design_decisions.rationale: The spec requires a distinct stderr line for timeouts ('timed out after <d> — escalating') vs generic implementer errors. The escalation logic (continue to next model) is identical for both. context.DeadlineExceeded is a sworn-internal signal (not a model.Error{Kind}), so S44's Kind-based routing will leave it on the existing 'escalate to next model' path.

design_decisions.choice: RunSliceOptions.ImplementTimeout with 0 meaning 'use default' — Go zero-value-as-unset convention for time.Duration
options: Zero value is valid (no timeout) and separate default-request sentinel
design_decisions.rationale: Go's idiomatic convention: zero-value time.Duration means unset. A negative value (-1) means 'no timeout' for opt-out. This is simpler than a separate sentinel constant.

design_decisions.choice: cancel() deferred per iteration — each attempt's context.WithTimeout creates a child context whose cancel is deferred immediately after creation
design_decisions.rationale: The parent context must not be leaked. A per-iteration defer ensures the deadline only bounds one implement attempt and the timer is released promptly.

design_decisions.choice: No timeout on RunSlice's own parent context — wrap only the implement step, not the verify step or commit/diff operations
options: Wrap entire for-loop body in timeout
design_decisions.rationale: The parent ctx may carry a broader deadline (e.g., from a CI job). Wrapping only implement.Run scopes the deadline to the agent loop, leaving verify/commit/diff unbounded-by-us.
